title: Buttery Light Filipino Chicken Noodles
description: A silky, post‑workout Filipino noodle dish that balances lightness with rich buttery flavor, featuring fragrant marjoram and halal-friendly ingredients.
instructions: In a medium bowl, mix the flour with 1/4 tsp salt and water until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 5 minutes until elastic, then cover and rest for 10 minutes.
Roll the rested dough thin and cut into long noodle strands, then rinse under cold water.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook the noodles for 3–4 minutes, then drain and set aside.
While the noodles cook, melt the butter in a pan over medium heat.
Add minced garlic, diced onion, and marjoram to the butter, sautéing until fragrant and translucent (about 2 minutes).
Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the chicken breast pieces, cooking until the chicken is fully cooked and reaches an internal temperature of 75°C (about 6–7 minutes).
Remove the chicken, slice into bite‑sized pieces, and return to the pan.
Add the cooked noodles and chicken back into the sauce, tossing gently to coat evenly.
Season with additional salt and pepper if needed, then serve hot.
Serve the dish immediately, optionally garnishing with extra marjoram or a squeeze of lime for brightness.
